_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval. Result of the ERCP the patient was referred for.

procedure.name: ERCP with cholangiography, dominant stricture assessment, biliary brushing cytology, and biliary stent placement
procedure.indication: PSC with new dominant stricture of the common hepatic duct and rising cholestatic labs (ALP 320 -> 480, total bilirubin 1.9); MRCP cannot exclude superimposed cholangiocarcinoma. Tissue sampling (brushings +/- FISH) to establish or exclude malignancy.
procedure.sedation: Monitored anesthesia care (propofol) with anesthesia support
procedure.prep_quality: N/A (biliary procedure); NPO confirmed, OTC fish oil held 5 days pre-procedure per instructions
procedure.extent_reached: Second portion of duodenum; major papilla identified and cannulated; selective deep biliary cannulation achieved

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: ERCP with cholangiography, biliary brushing cytology, and biliary stent placement. INDICATION: PSC with new dominant common hepatic duct stricture, rising cholestatic labs, and MRCP unable to exclude cholangiocarcinoma. SEDATION: MAC/propofol with anesthesia. ANTICOAGULATION: no prescription anticoagulant/antiplatelet; patient's OTC fish oil held pre-procedure; no reversal or bridging required. FINDINGS: The duodenoscope was advanced without difficulty to the major papilla. Selective deep biliary cannulation was achieved with a sphincterotome and guidewire. Cholangiography demonstrated the known PSC changes with multifocal intra- and extrahepatic strictures and beading. A dominant stricture of the common hepatic duct measuring approximately 2 cm in length was confirmed, with upstream intrahepatic ductal dilatation, corresponding to the MRCP finding and increased from prior. A biliary sphincterotomy was performed. The dominant stricture was traversed with a guidewire. Brush cytology of the dominant stricture was obtained with multiple passes (specimen sent for cytology and FISH). The stricture was dilated with a balloon, and a 10 Fr x 9 cm plastic biliary stent was placed across the dominant stricture with good bile drainage observed. No active bleeding at the sphincterotomy site. IMPRESSION: Dominant common hepatic duct stricture in PSC, brushed for cytology/FISH; balloon dilation and plastic biliary stent placed with good drainage. Cannot confirm or exclude cholangiocarcinoma at this time — awaiting cytology/FISH. Cytology and FISH pending.

findings.finding: Dominant stricture of common hepatic duct, ~2 cm, with upstream ductal dilatation
findings.action_taken: guidewire traversal, brush cytology (multiple passes), balloon dilation, plastic biliary stent placement

findings.finding: Multifocal intra- and extrahepatic strictures with beading (known PSC)
Biliary sphincterotomy site, no active bleeding

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Post-ERCP precautions reviewed including return for fever/chills, jaundice, or severe abdominal pain (post-ERCP pancreatitis/cholangitis). Resume ursodiol and mesalamine as before.

followup.recommendation: Review dominant stricture brush cytology + FISH; cancer cannot be excluded — communicate result to patient and hepatology and determine oncologic vs surveillance pathway
If cytology/FISH negative or indeterminate but clinical suspicion persists, consider repeat brushings or cholangioscopy with directed biopsy
Plastic biliary stent placed — schedule stent exchange/removal to avoid occlusion/cholangitis
Monitor for post-ERCP pancreatitis and cholangitis; return precautions given
Resume OTC fish oil per instructions; no prescription anticoagulant to resume
Confirm mesalamine 1.2 g daily and ursodiol 300 mg TID continued; ensure mesalamine reflected on active medication list

planted_followup_items.description: Dominant common hepatic duct stricture brush cytology + FISH pending — performed specifically to exclude cholangiocarcinoma in a PSC patient with a new dominant stricture, rising ALP/bilirubin, and MRCP that could not exclude malignancy. Result must be communicated and must drive the oncologic vs surveillance pathway.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: track cytology/FISH result; communicate to patient and hepatology; if positive/suspicious escalate to hepatobiliary/oncology for staging; if negative determine repeat sampling vs surveillance

planted_followup_items.description: Suspected cholangiocarcinoma remains unresolved (SUSPICION ONLY, not tissue-confirmed). A negative or indeterminate brushing does NOT exclude malignancy given brush cytology's low sensitivity — must not be dropped or falsely reassured.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: ensure definitive disposition of malignancy question; consider cholangioscopy with directed biopsy if suspicion persists despite non-diagnostic cytology

planted_followup_items.description: Plastic biliary stent (10 Fr x 9 cm) placed across dominant stricture — requires scheduled exchange/removal to prevent stent occlusion and cholangitis.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule stent exchange or removal ERCP in 8-12 weeks

planted_followup_items.description: Post-ERCP pancreatitis and cholangitis risk after sphincterotomy, dilation, and stenting; jaundice/fever/RUQ pain must prompt urgent evaluation.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: monitor 24-72h; return precautions reviewed; low threshold for evaluation and antibiotics if cholangitis

planted_followup_items.description: PSC-IBD dysplasia surveillance colonoscopy interval (annual) and ongoing PSC cholangiocarcinoma surveillance (MRCP/CA 19-9) to be maintained regardless of this result.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: ensure annual surveillance colonoscopy and PSC imaging surveillance are scheduled

planted_followup_items.description: Peri-procedure med reconciliation: OTC fish oil (patient's mislabeled 'blood thinner') may resume per instructions; no prescription anticoagulant exists to resume; confirm mesalamine 1.2 g daily is added to the active medication list (was missing).
planted_followup_items.expected_action: resume fish oil per instructions; reconcile and correct active medication list to include mesalamine

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a little scratchy and I'm burping up gas, but otherwise I'm okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/belching
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and gas are expected after ERCP and settle within 24-48h with fluids and rest.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I find out what the brush test showed? And when can I start my fish oil again?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending brush cytology/FISH result and resumption of OTC fish oil
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: cytology/FISH expected back ~2026-07-29 (day ~5); team will call with result and next steps. Fish oil (not a true blood thinner) may resume per instructions; no prescription anticoagulant to restart.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending pathology item and reconciles the fish-oil 'blood thinner' confusion.

contacts.verbatim: I've got really bad pain in the pit of my stomach going through to my back, and I keep throwing up. It started last night and it's getting worse.
contacts.clinical_signal: Severe epigastric pain radiating to the back with persistent vomiting after sphincterotomy/dilation/stenting — concerning for post-ERCP pancreatitis
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day/ED evaluation; check lipase and CBC, assess for post-ERCP pancreatitis; IV fluids and analgesia, admit as indicated.
contacts.rationale: Classic post-ERCP pancreatitis presentation is a true complication of this procedure and requires urgent evaluation, not reassurance.

contacts.verbatim: Do I need another procedure to take the stent out, and when would that be?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about the plastic biliary stent and the planned exchange/removal timeline
contacts.expected_action: Answer and schedule: the plastic biliary stent needs exchange/removal in 8-12 weeks to prevent occlusion/cholangitis; book the follow-up ERCP.
contacts.rationale: Answerable and schedulable from the follow-up plan; links to the stent exchange repeat_procedure item.
